title: Box 3 Tax Calculator
subtitle: Run the numbers in 90 seconds — savings, investments, second home, debts. No forms, no email signup, no ads buried between your figures.

assets.title: Your assets and debts
assets.wozNote: Use the WOZ value, not the market value. The WOZ value is typically 10–20% lower.
assets.primaryResidenceNote: Primary residence is in Box 1 and is not included in the Box 3 calculation.
assets.treatyNote: Real estate in a treaty country counts toward the tax-free allowance calculation, but Dutch Box 3 tax on that portion is waived (Bvdb 2001 art. 23).
assets.noTreatyNote: Even without a tax treaty, Dutch Box 3 tax on this property is waived proportionally (Bvdb 2001 art. 23). Note: if the other country also taxes the property, you bear that cost yourself.

debts.title: Debts
debts.mortgageNote: A mortgage on a second home is deductible in Box 3 (threshold €3,700, or €7,400 with a fiscal partner).

yearSelector.note2026: The 2026 savings and debt rates are provisional. Final rates will be published in January 2027.

about.title: About Box 3 tax
about.description: In the Netherlands, Box 3 tax is levied on your assets, such as savings, investments, and real estate (except your primary residence). The Dutch Tax Authority calculates Box 3 income according to the Bridging Legislation, which is based on the actual distribution of the different types of assets.
about.rates: For 2025, the following return rates apply:
about.taxRate: The tax rate for Box 3 in 2025 is 36%.
about.taxFreeAmount: The tax-free capital is € 57,000 per person (€ 114,000 for fiscal partners).
about.p1: Box 3 is the tax bracket for assets outside your primary home and your business. Savings, investments, a second property, rental properties: all of that falls into Box 3. Most people only really encounter it once they build up something above the tax-free threshold.
about.p2: What makes Box 3 unusual is that the tax authority does not look at what you actually earned. They apply a deemed return: a percentage you are assumed to have made, regardless of what actually happened. You then pay 36% tax on that deemed return.
about.p3: The deemed return is split by asset category. Savings get a low percentage (1.44% in 2025) because savings rates are low. Investments and real estate get a higher percentage (6.04%), as you are assumed to take on more risk and be rewarded for it. Debts are deductible at 2.61%.
about.p4: There is also an exemption: the tax-free allowance of €57,000 per person. If you are below that, you pay nothing. With a fiscal partner the combined threshold is €114,000. Many people with only savings are simply below that limit.
about.p5: This system has only been in its current form since 2023. The old system used one fixed deemed return for everything, and that led in December 2021 to the Supreme Court ruling that it was incompatible with European law. Since then a bridging scheme has been in force. A new system based on actual returns (accrual tax) starts on 1 January 2028.

items.q: Does my primary home count for Box 3?
items.a: No. Your primary home and the associated mortgage debt sit in Box 1, not Box 3. Only second homes, holiday properties and rental properties belong in Box 3. In the calculator here you tick 'primary home' for that property, and it gets cleanly excluded from the calculation.

items.q: How does the tax-free allowance work exactly?
items.a: The heffingvrij vermogen is a threshold: €57,000 per person in 2025, €114,000 jointly for fiscal partners. If your total Box 3 wealth after deducting debts sits below that, you owe nothing. Above it, you pay on the portion that exceeds the threshold — and that portion is then allocated pro rata across your asset categories. That last step is exactly the part most of us re-read twice — which is why this tool handles it for you.

You may deduct debts, but with a threshold. The first €3,700 doesn't count (€7,400 with a fiscal partner). Anything above is deducted using a fictitious yield of 2.61% in 2025. Important: mortgage debt on your primary home doesn't count here — that's Box 1. Mortgage debt on a second home does count, and the calculator here supports it.

Yes. Green investments are exempt from Box 3 up to €71,251 per person (€142,502 with a fiscal partner). On top of the exemption there's a 0.1% tax credit on the invested amount. The qualifying funds are specifically those with the Belastingdienst's 'groene belegging' designation — not every fund that calls itself sustainable qualifies. Ask your bank or broker explicitly before depositing.

items.q: What if I bought my second home in January?
items.a: For Box 3, what matters is what you own on 1 January — the peildatum. If you bought a second home on 5 January, it doesn't count for that entire tax year. It only enters from 1 January the following year. Conversely: if you sold on 30 December, it's already out of your base for that year. Designed to keep administration simple — but it does make timing of large transactions worth thinking about.

items.q: How is crypto treated in Box 3?
items.a: Crypto in principle falls under Box 3, classified as 'other assets'. You report the value at 1 January (converted to euros at that day's rate). The fictief rendement is that of investments — 6.04% in 2025. For staking yield and lending returns it gets more complex; case law is still evolving there. For straightforward crypto holders, declaring under other assets is the standard approach.

items.q: What if I don't trust the WOZ value?
items.a: You can file an objection against the WOZ — at your municipality, not the Belastingdienst. The window is six weeks after receiving the WOZ-beschikking. An independent appraisal often helps. For Box 3, the official WOZ value is used; adjust it via a successful objection and your assessment will follow automatically. For the tool here: enter the current official WOZ, not what you think it should be.

items.q: Does this calculator work for rented properties?
items.a: Yes, with one caveat. For Box 3 the property is valued at its WOZ value (not market value), with the fictief rendement of investments — 6.04% in 2025. For rented residential properties there's also been a leegwaarderatio correction since 2017: a discount on the WOZ based on the rent you charge. That correction isn't yet built into this calculator — it's on the list. For anyone with multiple rented properties: a tax advisor can optimise the leegwaarderatio for you.

items.q: Is this the same as my final tax assessment?
items.a: No. This is an estimate. The Belastingdienst does the final calculation based on your filed return, and may have small deviations — rounding rules, or specific items not modelled here. Use this as a guideline and a cross-check, not as a binding outcome. If the final assessment diverges sharply? Worth investigating — or worth dropping me an email.

From 1 January 2028 a new system replaces the bridging system. The base becomes aanwasbelasting: annual tax on the actual increase in value of your wealth — no more fictief rendement. For real estate and shares in startups there's an exception: profit tax, settled at the moment of sale. Until then the current system stays in force. The calculator here supports 2024, 2025 and 2026, plus a 2028 estimate under different return assumptions.
